pages.raw_text:
When asked why they are so
earnestly opposing the granting of
a saloon license in their part of the
city the temperance people of
Union park simply refer to the fol-
lowing announcement issued by a
saloon keeper at Joplin, Mo.


The front of the card bears his
name and address. He is Oscar
Pfotenhauer, and his saloon is To-
Gated at 901 Main st. On the re-
verse of the card is printed the fol-
lowing:


Friends and Neighbors: I am
grateful for past favors, and having
supplied my store with a fine line
of choice liquors, allow me to in
form you that | shall continue to
make drunkards, paupers and beg
CARS for the sober, industrious, re-
spectacle part of the community to
support My liquors will excite
riot. robbery and bloodshed.


They will diminish your COM
forts, increase your expenses and
shorten your life. | shall confident
ly recommend them as sure to null
tiply fatal accidents and increase
incurable diseases.


"They will deprive some of life,
others of reason, many OF char
acter, and all of peace. They will
make fathers fiends, wives widows,
children orphans, and all poor.


.v Ill n l.
ity, dissipation, ignorance, lewd-
ness and every other vice. | will
corrupt the minister of religion, OBJ
struct the gospel, defile the church
and cause as much temporal and
eternal death as | can. | will thus
accommodate the public'-it may
be at the loss of my never-dying
soul.


''But | have family to support,
the business pays and the public
encourages it. L have paid my IL
cense and the traffic is lawful, and
if | don't sell it somebody else
will I know the bible says, Thou
shalt not kill; no drunkard shall
enter the kingdom of heaven and
I do not expect the drunkard-maker
to fare any better, but | want an
easy living and | have resolved to
gather the wages of iniquity and
fatten on the ruin of my species.


Il shall, therefore, carry CT my
business and do my best to dimin-
ish the wealth of the nation and en-
danger the safety of the state. AS
my business flourishes in proper
tion to your sensibility and ignor-
ance | will do my best to prevent
moral purity and intellectual
growth.


Should you doubt my ability |
refer you to the pawnshOps, the
poorhouse. the police court, the
gallows, where you will find many
of my best customers have gone.
A sight of them will convince you
that | do what | say.


ALLOW me to inform you that
they are fools and that am an hon
est saloon keeper"
